title: Provision
$comment: A section/subsection/paragraph — the addressable 'function' a power/duty/veto attaches to (Annex A6.5b). provision_key is the primary key. Provision TEXT is NOT stored (decision 2026-07-13): the citation carries the legislation.gov.uk point-in-time url + version_date + a content_hash of the text extracted from (drift detection). outstanding_effects flags unapplied amendments (consolidation lag) — the 'incoming change' UX badge reads from it. Active from Spiral 2.

references.$comment: Outbound cross-references mined from the OPERATIVE text (decision #27). Objects, not bare provision_keys: a reference that points out of the model is exactly the lead we want to keep, and it has to carry its raw wording and target instrument even when no provision_key resolves. CLML marks up <Citation> only in amendment commentary, so these are parsed from the text itself.

raw.description: The reference as it appears in the text.

provision_key.description: Resolved target, when both instrument and section are known.

instrument_slug.description: Slug of the instrument referred to.

internal.description: True when the target is in the same Act.

in_model.description: False = an adjacent instrument we do not hold: a breadcrumb lead.

commenced_by.$comment: instrument_id of the commencement SI -> COMMENCED_BY

outstanding_effects.$comment: true = legislation.gov.uk lists unapplied amendments for this provision; consolidated text may not be current
